rulings.Case Title: GORDON ANDERSEN VS. 3M COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Defendant Ameron International Corporations Affirmative Defenses
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, November 8, 2019 in Department 503, Line 1. Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Adjudication of Defendant Ameron International Corporation's Affirmative Defenses is DENIED. Defendant has withdrawn defenses nos. 2, 3, 4, 5, 8, 9, 12, 18, 22, 23, 28, 29, 30, 33, 34, 36, 43, 47, 48, 49, and 50. As to all remaining defenses, Plaintiff failed to meet its burden of demonstrating that Defendant does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence regarding its affirmative defenses. (Defendant's Ex. O [Defendant's third supplemental responses to Plaintiff's special interrogatories nos. 30-32]; Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 843, 850-51.) The motion is denied as to all defenses on the same grounds as Plaintiff relies on undisputed material facts 1-10 and corresponding evidence for each defense. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
